reflection.title: spike-oidc-discovery-and-jwks
reflection.summary: Spiked VSO cluster JWT issuer/JWKS discovery against the live two-cluster kind lab, proved reachability from the Vault cluster, and documented the decision to use `jwks_url` (not `oidc_discovery_url`) for `auth/jwt-vso`, plus a required RBAC fix for anonymous discovery/JWKS access.
keyLearnings: - Default kind `iss` claim (`https://kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local`) is cluster-internal and unsafe/unreachable cross-cluster (it resolves to the *wrong* cluster's own API server from the other kind cluster). The discovery doc's self-reported `jwks_uri` is a Podman-bridge IP, also not reliably reachable.

- Decision locked: `auth/jwt-vso` config should use `jwks_url=https://host.containers.internal:6444/openid/v1/jwks` + `jwks_ca_pem=<VSO CA>` + `bound_issuer=<actual iss claim>` (bound_issuer is a string compare only, no reachability needed). Task 02 (kind issuer reconfiguration) is therefore NOT required.

- New required gap surfaced: default kubeadm/kind RBAC (`system:public-info-viewer`) does not grant unauthenticated access to `/.well-known/openid-configuration` or `/openid/v1/jwks`. Applied `oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding (granting `system:unauthenticated` GET on those two nonResourceURLs) directly to `kind-vso-lab` for this spike's evidence â€” it is NOT yet captured in any script and must be added idempotently to `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh` by task 04 or 05, or it's lost on cluster recreation.

- Practical tooling note: Vault's container image has no `curl`; busybox `wget` needs `SSL_CERT_FILE=<path>` (not `--ca-certificate`) for custom CA trust when testing TLS reachability via `kubectl exec`.

- VSO cluster CA for cross-cluster trust should be pulled from local kubeconfig `certificate-authority-data` (same pattern as `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`), not extracted from inside a pod.
blockers: none â€” all acceptance/validation criteria for this spike task were met and re-run successfully. The one open follow-up (formalizing the `oidc-discovery-reader` RBAC grant in `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) is explicitly flagged for tasks 04/05
not a blocker for task 01 itself.

reflection.title: implement-vso-jwt-auth-setup
reflection.summary: Implemented `scripts/configure-vso-jwt-auth.sh` (idempotent Vault JWT/OIDC auth setup for VSO, using jwks_url+bound_issuer per the Phase 1 spike decision, strict bound_audiences/bound_subject role binding, no token_reviewer_jwt) plus a 20-assertion validation test suite, and verified live against the real two-cluster lab (idempotency, correct login, wrong-audience/wrong-SA rejections).
keyLearnings: - - Critical gotcha found during live e2e testing: `vault read auth/.../config` in plain-text table format wraps multi-line PEM values (jwks_ca_pem) across many lines with no per-line key prefix on continuation lines â€” a naive `grep -vE '^jwks_ca_pem'` filter only strips the first line and leaks the rest of the CA cert. Fixed by using `vault read -format=json | jq 'del(.data.jwks_ca_pem, .data.jwt_validation_pubkeys, .data.oidc_discovery_ca_pem)'` instead, which added `jq` to the script's required commands. Any future script reading back JWT/OIDC config for display must follow this pattern.

- - Live e2e proof captured: correct `vso-demo` JWT (aud=vault) login succeeds and returns `["default" "mysecret"]` policies; `--audience not-vault` fails with "invalid audience (aud) claim"; `kubectl create token default -n vso-demo` (wrong SA) fails with "invalid subject (sub) claim"; `vault auth list` shows `jwt-vso/` and `kubernetes-vso/` coexisting without interference.

- - Test-writing gotcha: static "script never writes X" grep assertions must exclude the script's own explanatory comments/completion-message echoes (which legitimately mention `token_reviewer_jwt`/`oidc_discovery_url` to explain their absence) â€” match on the actual `key=value` write-argument pattern, not a bare substring.

- - Deliberately left the `oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding formalization (flagged by task 01's spike as needed in "task 04 or 05") to task 05 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`), since task 05's own spec is scoped to that exact file and task 04's spec/steps never mention RBAC. It remains applied out-of-band on the live cluster (confirmed present) but is not yet in any script â€” task 05 must add it.
- - Downstream tasks 05-08 (setup-vso-cluster.sh reviewer removal, VaultAuth method:jwt switch, Makefile targets, verify-two-cluster.sh JWT checks) are all still pending and untouched by this task.

role binds exact vso-demo/vso-demo subject + vault audience
no token_reviewer_jwt written
correct/wrong-audience/wrong-SA login proofs

keyLearnings: - Negative-login pattern under `set -euo pipefail`: `if kubectl_vault exec ... vault write ... jwt="${WRONG_JWT}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then fail_section ...; fi` â€” commands used as an `if` condition are exempt from `set -e`, so this correctly treats "login unexpectedly succeeded" as the failure branch without needing `|| true` tricks.

- Role/config strict-binding check reads `auth/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT}/role/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE}` via `vault read -format=json | jq` and compares `bound_audiences`/`bound_subject` exactly against `${VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE}` and `system:serviceaccount:${VSO_NAMESPACE}:vso-demo` â€” proves the config, not just that a role exists.

reflection.title: add-two-cluster-verification
reflection.summary: Added `scripts/verify-two-cluster.sh`, a 7-section end-to-end verification script proving the two-cluster VSO demo works (contexts, Vault/VSO placement in each cluster, network reachability, real Kubernetes-auth login, VaultStaticSecret sync, and rotation), wired it up as `make verify-two-cluster`, and added a unit test suite; live-verified against the real `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters including deliberate failure injection.
keyLearnings: - `kubectl create token <sa> --duration Xm` requires a minimum of 10 minutes (`may not specify a duration less than 10 minutes`) â€” used `10m` for the vso-demo SA JWT minted for the live auth/kubernetes-vso login check.

- Section 5 (kubernetes auth) does a *real* `vault write auth/kubernetes-vso/login role=vso-demo jwt=<minted-jwt>` and checks for `.auth.client_token` via `jq` â€” this proves actual auth works, not just that the mount exists.

- Section 6 compares `vault kv get -field=username kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` (Vault cluster) directly against the native Secret's base64-decoded value (VSO cluster) rather than hardcoding an expected string, so it's robust to whatever the current baseline is.

- Section 7 (rotation) always attempts to reset `kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` back to `BASELINE_USERNAME` even on the failure path (before calling `fail_section`), so a failed run never leaves the demo secret rotated; live-verified full round trip took ~6 poll attempts (~18s) with default `ROTATION_SLEEP=3`.

- Negative-placement checks (Vault absent from VSO cluster; VSO operator/CRDs/`vso-demo` namespace absent from Vault cluster) are asserted directly, matching the exit-criteria's "negative placement" requirement.
- `--check-only` only validates required commands + `require_contexts`, never touches a live cluster beyond that â€” matches the pattern used by every other script in `scripts/`.
- `--skip-rotation` flag lets `make verify-two-cluster`-style pre-demo checks run faster when the rotation window doesn't need re-proving every time.

- Full live run against real Podman-backed clusters took ~48s (with rotation); `--check-only` is near-instant; failure-path tests (bad `VAULT_ADDR`, bad `VSO_AUTH_ROLE`) were verified to fail at the correct section (4 and 5 respectively) with actionable diagnostics, and the baseline secret value (`larry`) was confirmed unchanged in both clusters afterward.

- Reused `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` directly (via `bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/check-vault-connectivity.sh"`) for section 4 instead of reimplementing the throwaway-curl-pod logic, keeping a single source of truth for that check.

reflection.title: add-two-cluster-kind-bootstrap
reflection.summary: Added `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (deterministic, idempotent Podman-backed kind two-cluster bootstrap) plus versioned kind config templates for `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab`, reusing task 03's `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` for shared context/port variables, and validated the full flow live (clusters created, reused on rerun, ports and cert SANs correct).
keyLearnings: - Task 03 (`scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`) had already landed in this repo (parallel DAG execution â€” 03 only depends on 01, not 02) before I started. I refactored `create-clusters.sh` to source it rather than introducing a duplicate `scripts/lib/ports.sh`, reusing its `VAULT_CONTEXT`, `VSO_CONTEXT`, `VAULT_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `VSO_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST`, `VAULT_HOST_PORT`, `VSO_API_HOST_PORT`, and `require_commands`. Downstream tasks (04+) should keep sourcing `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as the single source of truth for these names/ports â€” I only added `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820` to it (task 05 will create the actual Vault NodePort Service on this port).

- Kind config templates live at `scripts/kind/*.yaml.tmpl` using literal `${VAR}` placeholders substituted by `sed` in `create-clusters.sh` (no `envsubst` dependency â€” not guaranteed present on all machines). Edit the templates + `two-cluster-env.sh` together if the port contract changes.

- **Critical environment finding**: `kind get clusters` (and anything using kind's podman provider list path, e.g. `preflight_two_cluster_network` in `two-cluster-env.sh`) is broken with podman client 6.0.0 â€” `podman ps --format '{{index .Labels "key"}}'` fails because podman 6's ps format renders `.Labels` as a flat comma string, not a map, so `index` fails with `exit status 125`. I upgraded `kind` 0.29.0 â†’ 0.32.0 via brew, which did NOT fix this (it's a podman-side format change, not a kind-version issue). **`create-clusters.sh` therefore does NOT use `kind get clusters` for idempotency** â€” instead it attempts `kind create cluster` directly and treats kind's own `"node(s) already exist"` stderr message as the reuse signal. Any future script that calls `kind get clusters` on this machine/podman version will also fail; prefer `kubectl config get-contexts <ctx>` or `podman ps --filter label=...` (without `index`) for cluster-existence checks instead.

- Verified live: `kind-vault-lab` maps container NodePort 30820 â†’ host port 8200; `kind-vso-lab`'s API server listens on host port 6444 with `host.containers.internal` (plus `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`) baked into the serving cert's SAN list (confirmed via `openssl s_client`/`x509`). Both `kind-vault-lab` and `kind-vso-lab` contexts exist in kubeconfig; script never changes the caller's current-context.
- Both real kind clusters (`vault-lab`, `vso-lab`) are left running in this environment for downstream tasks (04+) to build on.

reflection.title: refactor-vso-cluster-setup
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, a context-aware script that installs the Vault Secrets Operator, creates the `vso-demo` namespace with `vso-demo`/`vault-token-reviewer` service accounts, and grants TokenReview RBAC (`system:auth-delegator`) â€” all scoped exclusively to `VSO_CONTEXT` (`kind-vso-lab`) via the shared `kubectl_vso`/`helm_vso` wrappers, with no Vault auth config or VSO CRDs applied. keyLearnings: - Followed the `--check-only` pattern established by `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (task 02): validates `require_commands kubectl helm` and `require_context "$VSO_CONTEXT"` before doing any real work, and exits early with `--check-only` for safe testing.

- RBAC choice: bound `vault-token-reviewer` SA to the built-in `system:auth-delegator` ClusterRole (same pattern `create_vault.sh` used for the Vault SA itself) â€” this grants both TokenReview and SubjectAccessReview, the standard binding for Kubernetes-auth reviewer identities. reflection.title: configure-vso-kubernetes-auth
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`, an idempotent, context-explicit script that enables and configures a dedicated `auth/kubernetes-vso` Vault Kubernetes auth mount validated against the VSO cluster's API server, writes the least-privilege `vso-demo` role, and was validated live end-to-end (auth enable, config, role write, and a real cross-cluster JWT login returning the `mysecret` policy).
keyLearnings: - Reviewer JWT is minted via `kubectl create token vault-token-reviewer -n vso-demo --duration 8760h` (using `kubectl_vso`), not a static Secret-bound SA token â€” simpler on modern Kubernetes and documented as a demo-only limitation (not auto-refreshed; re-running the script mints a fresh one and rewrites `auth/kubernetes-vso/config`, which is otherwise a safe, idempotent operation).

- The VSO cluster's API server CA is read directly from the local kubeconfig (`kubectl config view --raw` for `VSO_CONTEXT`'s cluster entry, base64-decoded), not from inside a VSO-cluster pod â€” this works because kind's kubeconfig embeds the same CA that signs the API server cert whose SANs include `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST` (set up in task 02/03). This is the one intentional bare `kubectl` call in the script (context-agnostic by design), everything else goes through `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`.

- `vault write auth/<mount>/config kubernetes_ca_cert="$MULTILINE_PEM"` works fine passed straight through `kubectl exec ... -- vault write ...` (no `sh -c`) since kubectl exec forwards argv elements as-is without shell re-parsing, so embedded newlines in a quoted bash variable survive intact.

- `auth/kubernetes-vso/role/vso-demo` is bound only to SA `vso-demo` in namespace `vso-demo` (VSO_NAMESPACE) with `policies=mysecret` only (no `default` policy on the role â€” Vault still adds Vault's own `default` policy automatically at login, which is expected and fine, verified in `token_policies=["default","mysecret"]` from an actual login response).

- Live-verified against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters (already set up by tasks 04/06): `vault auth list` shows `kubernetes-vso/` alongside the untouched same-cluster `kubernetes/` mount; a fresh SA token minted from `kind-vso-lab`'s `vso-demo` SA logs in successfully via `auth/kubernetes-vso/login` and receives the `mysecret` policy; re-running the script is fully idempotent (mount stays enabled, config/role rewritten cleanly, login still works afterward).

- Follows the same script conventions as tasks 02/04/06: sources `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, supports `--check-only`/`-h`/`--help`, uses `require_commands`/`require_contexts`, and ships a matching `scripts/tests/*-validation.sh` unit test (10/10 passing) that also greps for accidental bare `kubectl` calls and any reference to the same-cluster `auth/kubernetes/` mount.

reflection.summary: Rewrote the top-level `Makefile` to add explicit two-cluster (Podman-backed kind) targets â€” `clusters`, `setup-vault`, `setup-vso`, `configure-vso-auth`, `vso-apply`, `check-vault-connectivity` â€” and an orchestrating `setup` target, while cleanly splitting old single-cluster targets (`verify`, `status`, `logs-otel`, `logs-agent`) from new two-cluster ones (`vso-verify`, `vso-status`, `logs-vso`) that use explicit `--context` flags.

- Important Make gotcha discovered: `## comment` help text is extracted via `grep`/`awk` over the raw `$(MAKEFILE_LIST)` file text, which is NOT subject to make's `$(VAR)` expansion (that only happens in recipe lines evaluated by make itself, not in comments read by a grep subprocess). Any `$(VAULT_CONTEXT)`-style references in help comments render as literal `$(VAULT_CONTEXT)` â€” so help text must reference variable names as plain text (e.g. "(VAULT_CONTEXT)") rather than expecting substitution.

- `make setup` runs `create-clusters.sh` â†’ `setup-vault-cluster.sh` â†’ `setup-vso-cluster.sh` â†’ `configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh` â†’ `apply-vso-demo.sh` in strict order (each depends on the previous per the task dependency chain 02â†’...â†’08); this ordering is required and was live-verified end-to-end on real Podman-backed clusters, including a full idempotent re-run.

reflection.title: spike-oidc-discovery-and-jwks
reflection.summary: Spiked VSO cluster JWT issuer/JWKS discovery against the live two-cluster kind lab, proved reachability from the Vault cluster, and documented the decision to use `jwks_url` (not `oidc_discovery_url`) for `auth/jwt-vso`, plus a required RBAC fix for anonymous discovery/JWKS access.
keyLearnings: - Default kind `iss` claim (`https://kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local`) is cluster-internal and unsafe/unreachable cross-cluster (it resolves to the *wrong* cluster's own API server from the other kind cluster). The discovery doc's self-reported `jwks_uri` is a Podman-bridge IP, also not reliably reachable.

- Decision locked: `auth/jwt-vso` config should use `jwks_url=https://host.containers.internal:6444/openid/v1/jwks` + `jwks_ca_pem=<VSO CA>` + `bound_issuer=<actual iss claim>` (bound_issuer is a string compare only, no reachability needed). Task 02 (kind issuer reconfiguration) is therefore NOT required.

- New required gap surfaced: default kubeadm/kind RBAC (`system:public-info-viewer`) does not grant unauthenticated access to `/.well-known/openid-configuration` or `/openid/v1/jwks`. Applied `oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding (granting `system:unauthenticated` GET on those two nonResourceURLs) directly to `kind-vso-lab` for this spike's evidence â€” it is NOT yet captured in any script and must be added idempotently to `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh` by task 04 or 05, or it's lost on cluster recreation.

- Practical tooling note: Vault's container image has no `curl`; busybox `wget` needs `SSL_CERT_FILE=<path>` (not `--ca-certificate`) for custom CA trust when testing TLS reachability via `kubectl exec`.

- VSO cluster CA for cross-cluster trust should be pulled from local kubeconfig `certificate-authority-data` (same pattern as `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`), not extracted from inside a pod.
blockers: none â€” all acceptance/validation criteria for this spike task were met and re-run successfully. The one open follow-up (formalizing the `oidc-discovery-reader` RBAC grant in `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) is explicitly flagged for tasks 04/05
not a blocker for task 01 itself.

reflection.title: implement-vso-jwt-auth-setup
reflection.summary: Implemented `scripts/configure-vso-jwt-auth.sh` (idempotent Vault JWT/OIDC auth setup for VSO, using jwks_url+bound_issuer per the Phase 1 spike decision, strict bound_audiences/bound_subject role binding, no token_reviewer_jwt) plus a 20-assertion validation test suite, and verified live against the real two-cluster lab (idempotency, correct login, wrong-audience/wrong-SA rejections).
keyLearnings: - - Critical gotcha found during live e2e testing: `vault read auth/.../config` in plain-text table format wraps multi-line PEM values (jwks_ca_pem) across many lines with no per-line key prefix on continuation lines â€” a naive `grep -vE '^jwks_ca_pem'` filter only strips the first line and leaks the rest of the CA cert. Fixed by using `vault read -format=json | jq 'del(.data.jwks_ca_pem, .data.jwt_validation_pubkeys, .data.oidc_discovery_ca_pem)'` instead, which added `jq` to the script's required commands. Any future script reading back JWT/OIDC config for display must follow this pattern.

- - Live e2e proof captured: correct `vso-demo` JWT (aud=vault) login succeeds and returns `["default" "mysecret"]` policies; `--audience not-vault` fails with "invalid audience (aud) claim"; `kubectl create token default -n vso-demo` (wrong SA) fails with "invalid subject (sub) claim"; `vault auth list` shows `jwt-vso/` and `kubernetes-vso/` coexisting without interference.

- - Test-writing gotcha: static "script never writes X" grep assertions must exclude the script's own explanatory comments/completion-message echoes (which legitimately mention `token_reviewer_jwt`/`oidc_discovery_url` to explain their absence) â€” match on the actual `key=value` write-argument pattern, not a bare substring.

- - Deliberately left the `oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding formalization (flagged by task 01's spike as needed in "task 04 or 05") to task 05 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`), since task 05's own spec is scoped to that exact file and task 04's spec/steps never mention RBAC. It remains applied out-of-band on the live cluster (confirmed present) but is not yet in any script â€” task 05 must add it.
- - Downstream tasks 05-08 (setup-vso-cluster.sh reviewer removal, VaultAuth method:jwt switch, Makefile targets, verify-two-cluster.sh JWT checks) are all still pending and untouched by this task.

reflection.title: update-two-cluster-verification
reflection.summary: Updated `scripts/verify-two-cluster.sh`'s auth section to authenticate via `auth/jwt-vso` (JWT/OIDC) with a positive `vso-demo`/audience-`vault` login, mandatory wrong-audience and wrong-service-account negative logins, and a strict claim-binding role review, plus an updated final summary; updated its static test suite to match, and verified everything live end-to-end.
keyLearnings: - Negative-login pattern under `set -euo pipefail`: `if kubectl_vault exec ... vault write ... jwt="${WRONG_JWT}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then fail_section ...; fi` â€” commands used as an `if` condition are exempt from `set -e`, so this correctly treats "login unexpectedly succeeded" as the failure branch without needing `|| true` tricks.

- Role/config strict-binding check reads `auth/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT}/role/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE}` via `vault read -format=json | jq` and compares `bound_audiences`/`bound_subject` exactly against `${VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE}` and `system:serviceaccount:${VSO_NAMESPACE}:vso-demo` â€” proves the config, not just that a role exists.

- All minted JWT shell variables (`VSO_SA_JWT`, `WRONG_AUD_JWT`, `WRONG_SA_JWT`) are `unset` right after use and never echoed directly, per the "don't log full JWT values" requirement; only Vault's own login response is ever printed on failure.
